Business Content 1: In the state of Maryland, effective January 22, 2026, there is a requirement related to the Proof of Loss category. If a claim is submitted late, there is an extension available for submission that applies after the 90 to 365 day deadline has expired. This extension allows for submission up to one year after the deadline, but it is only valid if it can be demonstrated that earlier proof of loss was not possible, except in cases where there was a lack of legal capacity.

Business Content 2: In the state of Maryland, starting from January 22, 2026, individuals are required to provide ongoing proof of loss during the benefit period. This proof must be submitted as requested by Prudential and must be furnished as often as Prudential reasonably requires. The satisfactory proof must be received within 30 days of a request by Prudential. Prudential will deny claims or stop sending benefit payments if the appropriate information, proof, or authorization, satisfactory to Prudential, is not submitted. For claims related to Covered Critical Illness, Prudential may request proof of continuing Covered Critical Illness or that the individual is under the Regular Care of a Doctor. This proof must be provided at the individual's expense. Satisfactory proof of claim must show that the individual is under the Regular Care of a Doctor, appropriate documentation of Weekly or Monthly Earnings, and appropriate documentation that the individual is not working at any job during the Elimination Period. Satisfactory proof must be submitted no later than 90 to 365 days after the Elimination Period ends, and if not possible, it must be given no later than 1 to 3 years after the time satisfactory proof is otherwise required, except in the absence of legal capacity. If notice is not given in the specified time, the claim will not be invalidated. The proof may include medical information, financial information, and any other items Prudential may reasonably require to document Disability or to determine eligibility for benefits. The date the individual fails to submit satisfactory proof of a continued Covered Critical Illness satisfactory to Prudential will also be a condition for stopping benefits.

Notice of a claim should be sent in a format satisfactory to Prudential within 30 – 365 days after the date Your Disability begins or as soon thereafter as is reasonably possible. You must provide satisfactory proof of a continued Covered Critical Illness satisfactory to Prudential within 30 days of a request by Prudential.
